name: nasm_quote_filename
documentation: It is considered necessary if any one of these is true: 1. The filename contains control characters; 2. The filename starts or ends with a space or quote mark; 3. The filename contains more than one space in a row; 4. The filename is empty. The filename is returned in a newly allocated buffer.

name: free_mmacro
documentation: Free an MMacro

name: set_text_free
signature: static Token *set_text_free(struct Token *t, char *text, unsigned int len)
documentation: Set the text field to the existing pre-allocated string, either taking over or freeing the allocation in the process.

name: dup_text
documentation: Allocate a new buffer containing a copy of the text field of the token.

name: make_tok_num
documentation: Create a numeric token, with possible - token in front

name: make_tok_num_radix
signature: static Token * make_tok_num_radix(Token *next, int64_t val, char radix, bool uns)
documentation: Create a numeric token with specified radix and signedness; prefix the number with 0<radix> if a radix letter is specified, otherwise generate a decimal constant without prefix.

name: make_tok_qstr_len
signature: static Token *make_tok_qstr_len(Token *next, const char *str, size_t len)
documentation: Create a quoted string token

name: make_tok_char
documentation: Create a single-character operator token

name: new_Token
signature: static Token *new_Token(Token * next, enum token_type type, const char *text, size_t txtlen)
documentation: this function creates a new Token and passes a pointer to it back to the caller. It sets the type, text, and next pointer elements.

name: new_Token_free
signature: static Token *new_Token_free(Token * next, enum token_type type, char *text, size_t txtlen)
documentation: Same as new_Token(), but text belongs to the new token and is either taken over or freed. This function MUST be called with valid txt and txtlen, unlike new_Token().

name: steal_Token
documentation: This *transfers* the content from one token to another, leaving the next pointer of the latter intact. Unlike dup_Token(), the old token is destroyed, except for its next pointer, and the text pointer allocation, if any, is simply transferred.

name: zap_white
documentation: Delete any whitespace

name: free_tlist
documentation: Free a linked list of tokens.

name: free_line
documentation: Free a line

name: free_llist
documentation: Free a linked list of lines.

name: free_tlist_array
documentation: Free an array of linked lists of tokens

name: dup_tlist
documentation: Duplicate a linked list of tokens.

name: dup_tlistn
signature: static Token *dup_tlistn(const Token *list, size_t cnt, Token ***tailp)
documentation: Duplicate a linked list of tokens with a maximum count

name: dup_tlist_reverse
documentation: Duplicate a linked list of tokens in reverse order

name: new_mmacro
documentation: Allocate a new MMacro. This does not claim a refcount; the appropriate get_mmacro() calls need to be added.

name: free_mmacro_list
documentation: Unconditionally free a list of MMacros; used on final cleanup

name: free_smacro_members
documentation: Clear or free an SMacro

name: free_smacro
documentation: Free an SMacro

name: inc_fopen_search
signature: static FILE *inc_fopen_search(const char *file, char **slpath, enum incopen_mode *omp, enum file_flags fmode)
documentation: This is conducts a full pathname search

name: inc_fopen
signature: static FILE *inc_fopen(const char *file, struct strlist *dhead, const struct file_hash_entry **found_fhe, enum incopen_mode omode, enum file_flags fmode)

name: tlist_filename
documentation: Expand a token list that is expected to contain a filename string. Returns a new token containing a TOK_INTERNAL_STR with the given filename, or NULL on error. If the argument "*otp" is set, set that to point to the actual quoted string token.

name: pp_do_pathsearch
documentation: This implements the %pathsearch directive and %pathsearch() function. Returns a new token.

name: pp_do_depend
documentation: This implements the %depend directive and the %depend() function. It returns a stolen copy of the original string token after skipping leading spaces, or NULL on error. Returns a new token.

name: count_mmac_params
documentation: in a %macro definition line. Note that we need space in the params array for parameter 0 being a possible captured label as well as the final NULL. Returns a pointer to the pointer to a terminal comma if present; used to drop an empty terminal argument for legacy reasons.

name: smacro_expand_default
signature: static Token * smacro_expand_default(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: Default smacro expansion routine: just returns a copy of the expansion list.

name: expand_one_smacro
documentation: to point to the first token of the expansion, and *tpp updated to point to the next pointer of the last token of the expansion. If the expansion is empty, *tpp will be unchanged but **tpp will be advanced past the macro call. Return the macro expanded, or NULL if no expansion took place.

name: expand_smacro_with_params
signature: static Token * expand_smacro_with_params(SMacro *m, Token *mstart, Token **params, int nparam, Token ***epp)
documentation: Expand one single-line macro instance given a specific macro and a specific set of parameters. Returns a pointer to the expansion, and the pointer *epp pointing to the next pointer of the last token of the expansion; if the expansion is empty return NULL and *epp is unchanged. mstart is the token containing the token name *as invoked*.

name: use_mmacro
documentation: This is called from find_mmacro_in_list() after finding a suitable macro.

name: stdmac_ptr
signature: static Token * stdmac_ptr(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)

name: stdmac_default
signature: static Token * stdmac_default(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)

name: stdmac_strcat
signature: static Token * stdmac_strcat(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %strcat() function

name: stdmac_hs2b
signature: static Token * stdmac_hs2b(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %hs2b() function

name: stdmac_b2hs
signature: static Token * stdmac_b2hs(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %b2hs() function

name: stdmac_chr
signature: static Token * stdmac_chr(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %chr() function

name: stdmac_sel
signature: static Token * stdmac_sel(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %sel()

name: stdmac_cond
signature: static Token * stdmac_cond(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %cond()

name: stdmac_selbits
signature: static Token * stdmac_selbits(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
documentation: %selbits()

name: stdmac_num
signature: static Token * stdmac_num(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)
documentation: %num() function

name: stdmac_abs
signature: static Token * stdmac_abs(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)
documentation: %abs() function

name: stdmac_map
signature: static Token * stdmac_map(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)
documentation: %map() function

name: stdmac_realpath
signature: static Token * stdmac_realpath(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)

name: stdmac_env
signature: static Token * stdmac_env(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)

name: nasm_quote
documentation: Create a NASM quoted string in newly allocated memory. Update the *lenp parameter with the output length (sans final NUL).

name: string_transform
signature: size_t string_transform(char *str, size_t len, char **out, enum strfunc func)
documentation: Apply a specific string transform and return it in a nasm_malloc'd buffer, returning the length. On error, returns (size_t)-1 and no buffer is allocated.

name: nasm_z_alloc
signature: static void *nasm_z_alloc(void *opaque, unsigned int items, unsigned int size)
documentation: read line from standard macros set, if there no more left -- return NULL

name: nasm_delete
documentation: This is broken on platforms where there are pointers which don't match void * in their internal layout. It unfortunately also loses any "const" part of the argument, although hopefully the compiler will warn in that case.
macro_value: do { \ void **_pp = (void **)&(p); \ nasm_assert_pointer(p); \ nasm_free(*_pp); \ *_pp = NULL; \ } while (0)

name: expand_buffer
signature: static void *expand_buffer(void *buf, size_t *bufsizep, size_t datasize, size_t elemsize)
documentation: Make sure there is enough space for at least one additional element

name: nasm_realloc
documentation: Unlike the system realloc, we do *not* allow size == 0 to be the equivalent to free(); we guarantee returning a non-NULL pointer. The check for calling malloc() is theoretically redundant, but be paranoid about the system library...

name: nasm_vaxprintf
signature: void *nasm_vaxprintf(size_t extra, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
documentation: The length of the string (with or without the final NUL) is available by calling nasm_last_string_{len,size}() afterwards. nasm_[v]axprintf() are similar, but allocates a user-defined amount of storage before the string, and returns a pointer to the allocated buffer. The size of that area is not included in the value returned by nasm_last_string_size().

name: hash_free
documentation: Free the hash itself. Doesn't free the data elements; use hash_iterate() to do that first, if needed. This function is normally used when the hash data entries are either freed separately, or compound objects which can't be freed in a single operation.

name: hash_free_all
documentation: Frees the hash *and* all data elements. This is applicable only in the case where the data element is a single allocation. If the second argument is false, the key string is part of the data allocation or belongs to an allocation which will be freed separately, if it is true the keys are also freed.

name: filename_set_extension
documentation: Add/modify a filename extension. Returns a newly allocated string buffer. If the extension is not an empty string, the first character is replaced by the appropriate extsep for the filesystem.

name: strlist_free
documentation: Free a string list. Sets the pointed to pointer to NULL.

name: elf_make_section
signature: static struct elf_section * elf_make_section(char *name, int type, int flags, uint64_t align)

name: ol_seg_alloc
documentation: Allocate a section index and add a section, subsection, or external symbol to the section-by-index table. If the index provided is zero, allocate a new index via seg_alloc().

name: _ol_get_sect
signature: struct ol_sect *_ol_get_sect(const char *name, size_t ssize, size_t rsize)
documentation: Find a section or create a new section structure if it does not exist and allocate it an index value via seg_alloc().

name: _ol_new_subsection
documentation: Start a new subsection for the given section. At the moment, once a subsection has been created, it is not possible to revert to an earlier subsection. ol_sect_by_index() will return the main section structure. Returns the new section index. This is used to prevent the front end from optimizing across subsection boundaries.

name: _ol_new_sym
signature: struct ol_sym *_ol_new_sym(const char *name, const struct ol_loc *v, uint32_t flags, size_t size)
documentation: If flags has OF_GLOBAL set, add it to the global symbol hash for the containing section if applicable. If flags has OF_IMPSEC set, allocate a segment index for it via seg_alloc() unless v->index is already set, and add it to the section by index list.

name: ol_new_sym
signature: static inline O_Symbol *ol_new_sym(const char *name, const struct ol_loc *v, uint32_t flags)

name: obj_clear
documentation: static bool obj_uppercase; /* Flag: all names in uppercase static bool obj_use32; /* Flag: at least one segment is 32-bit static bool obj_nodepend; /* Flag: don't emit file dependencies Clear an ObjRecord structure. (Never reallocates). To simplify reuse of ObjRecord's, .type, .ori and .parm are not cleared.

name: obj_emit
documentation: Emit an ObjRecord structure. (Never reallocates). The record is written out proceeded (recursively) by its previous part (if any) and followed (recursively) by its child (if any). The previous part and the child are freed. The main ObjRecord is cleared, not freed.

name: obj_emit2
documentation: Commit and Emit a record. (Never reallocates).

name: obj_new
documentation: Allocate and clear a new ObjRecord; Also sets .ori to ori_null

name: obj_bump
documentation: Advance to the next record because the existing one is full or its x_size is incompatible. Any uncommitted data is moved into the next record.
